prompt: The interface is used as a marker but also adds unintended methods. What is the bug?
choices: A true marker interface has no methods; adding save() makes it a functional interface instead of a pure marker, requiring all implementors to provide save() even if they don't need it
backup() should cast o to Saveable before checking instanceof
Interface methods are optional for implementors in Java 9+
explanation: A marker interface (like Serializable, Cloneable) has no methods — it only marks a type for special treatment. Adding save() forces every implementing class to provide it, conflating two concerns. Fix: keep the marker interface empty; define a separate Persistable interface with save() for classes that need it.
